current_implemented_build: V55.5 TrustMap CSS and Visual Object Cleanup
patch_type: TrustMap CSS externalization and visual object cleanup over V55.4 registry-rendered TrustMap
release_chain_status: V55.5 is the current implemented build. It loads after V55.4 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It preserves the registry-rendered radar-constellation TrustMap, thick neon-blue CyberShield Core shield boundary, baseline Layer 1 operating domains, Purpose Protocol integration, and no-new-top-level-tabs rule. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V55.5. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
core_thesis: CyberShield evaluates whether the information behind a critical action or claim can be trusted before the organization acts, cites, shares, briefs, teaches, or relies on it.
trustmap_doctrine: The CyberShield TrustMap is a radar-constellation interface, not a process-flow diagram. CyberShield Core is a thick neon-blue shield boundary and trust kernel, not a normal node. Complexity lives at the edge. Trust is established at the center. Layer 1 domains are neon-blue graphical trust objects. Baseline Layer 1 domains must stay visible.
v55_5_changes: externalizes active v554 TrustMap visual styles into a maintainable stylesheet
preserves thick neon-blue CyberShield Core shield boundary
keeps no black bevel around CyberShield Core
keeps Internet Trust Engine as a documented future requirement track, not a live feature

trustmap_stylesheet.boundary: Legacy injected CSS may still exist as fallback from prior modules, but V55.5 stylesheet is now the maintainable visual source for active v554 TrustMap classes.

purpose_protocol_track.status: implemented as static advisory scaffold in V55 and preserved in V55.5
purpose_protocol_track.supporting_principle: Purpose is not governance until it can cause a refusal.
purpose_protocol_track.priority_scenario: Vendor payment destination change: if banking details changed within 30 days, payment approval is refused unless current banking verification and controller approval are present.
purpose_protocol_track.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. No live banking verification, live payment blocking, live controller workflow, backend email, CRM, ticketing, or enforcement.

avoid_framing: artifact-level trust score as the MVP anchor
internet_trust_engine_future_track.sequence_note: Do not implement before TrustMap registry, visual stabilization, interaction reliability, model transparency, and evidence/proof layers are stable.

recommended_next_build.label: V55.6 TrustMap Interaction Reliability
goals: ensure every TrustMap object explains, routes, calculates, downloads, or triggers next step
add richer Object View and detail-panel behavior
keep Internet Trust Engine as future track until trust/evidence layers are ready

implemented_release_train.v52: Model Registry Foundation and Human-First Executive Control View
implemented_release_train.v52.1: TrustMap Visual Restoration and Layer Patch
implemented_release_train.v52.2: Guided Onboarding, Routed Dashboard, and Layered TrustMap Patch

implemented_release_train.v52.7: TrustMap Navigation and Report Output System
implemented_release_train.v53: Trust Model and Deep Scenario Spine Build
implemented_release_train.v55.2: TrustMap Core Boundary and Constellation Polish
implemented_release_train.v55.3: TrustMap Registry and Visual Object Stabilization
implemented_release_train.v55.5: TrustMap CSS and Visual Object Cleanup

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live evidence retrieval, internet claim verification, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Legacy injected CSS still exists as fallback in prior modules
Layer 1 SVG/CSS icons are prototype approximations
reports download as text files rather than branded PDFs
contact capture is client-side only and not stored
scoring and trend lines are demo-directional and not statistically validated
Internet Trust Engine is documented as a future requirement track but is not yet implemented
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
